---
title: Dev setup
description: Local development environment for the Studyflow Modeler
---

This page is for contributors working on the modeler codebase or the docs site.

## Prerequisites

- Node.js (LTS)
- [Quarto CLI](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/) (for the docs site)

## Clone and install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/behaverse/studyflow-modeler.git
cd studyflow-modeler
npm install
```

## Common tasks

```bash
npm run dev           # start the modeler app with live reload
npm run build         # build the modeler app to dist/
npm run docs          # preview the docs site with live reload
npm run docs:build    # render the docs to dist/docs/
npm test              # run unit tests
npx playwright test   # run end-to-end tests
```

## Repository layout

- `src/modeler/` — bpmn-js–based diagram editor (palette, inspector, render, extensions).
- `src/runtime/` — experimental runtime for executing studyflows.
- `src/assets/schemas/` — moddle YAML schemas. See [Moddle schemas](schemas.qmd) for the format.
- `src/assets/locales/` — UI translations.
- `docs/` — Quarto site for this documentation.
- `tests/` — Playwright end-to-end tests.

## Contributing

Issues and pull requests are welcome on [GitHub](https://github.com/behaverse/studyflow-modeler). For larger changes (new element types, schema extensions), please open an issue first to discuss.
